lede: A look at June 29 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Sam Lavagnino and Shmuly Yanklowitz, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Islamic State
events.desc: The Islamic State of Iraq and the Levant self-declares its caliphate in Syria and northern Iraq.

events.title: Space Shuttle program
events.desc: Space Shuttle program: STS-71 Mission (Atlantis) docks with the Russian space station Mir for the first time.

events.title: Isabel Perón
events.desc: Vice President Isabel Perón assumes powers and duties as Acting President of Argentina, while her husband President Juan Perón is terminally ill.

events.title: Miss Universe
events.desc: The first Miss Universe pageant is held. Armi Kuusela from Finland wins the title of Miss Universe 1952.

events.title: Hyde Park Township, Illinois
events.desc: Hyde Park and several other Illinois townships vote to be annexed by Chicago, forming the largest United States city in area and second largest in population at the time.

events.title: George Edward Gouraud
events.desc: George Edward Gouraud records Handel's Israel in Egypt onto a phonograph cylinder, thought for many years to be the oldest known recording of music.

events.title: Russo-Turkish War (1806–1812)
events.desc: Russo-Turkish War: Admiral Dmitry Senyavin destroys the Ottoman fleet in the Battle of Athos.

events.title: 1764 Woldegk tornado
events.desc: One of the strongest tornadoes in history strikes Woldegk, Germany, killing one person while leveling numerous mansions with winds estimated greater than 300 miles per hour (480 km/h).

events.title: Globe Theatre
events.desc: The Globe Theatre in London, built by William Shakespeare's playing company, the Lord Chamberlain's Men, burns to the ground.

events.title: Dordrecht
events.desc: The Dutch city of Dordrecht is devastated by fire

births.name: Sean Bergin
births.line: South African-Dutch saxophonist and flute player (died 2012)

deaths.text: Yong Nyuk Lin, Singaporean politician, Singaporean Minister of Health
Marjorie Linklater, Scottish campaigner for the arts and environment of Orkney
Paul Troje, German politician, Mayor of Marburg

observances.text: Christian feast day: Mary, mother of John Mark
Christian feast day: Feast of Saints Peter and Paul (Western Christianity), and its related observances: Haro Wine Festival (Haro, La Rioja)
Christian feast day: Feast of Saints Peter and Paul (Western Christianity), and its related observances: l-Imnarja (Malta)
Independence Day (Seychelles), celebrates the independence of Seychelles from the United Kingdom in 1976.
